summary: Tested (a) new 'know_it_sheet' content type creation + type switching, and (b) grade-scoped content move and content_count behaviour. Backend: 3/3 pytest cases pass. Frontend: admin Content Management → Grade 1 → Subtopics shows amber 'Grade filter active' note (data-testid=move-subtopic-grade-note) in Move dialog; subtopic 'X content items' labels render correctly. Edit Content dialog under Step 3 Lesson Plan shows all 6 content-type picker cards including data-testid=content-type-pick-know_it_sheet with current type highlighted.
critical_code_review_comments: GET /api/content/topics: admin role does NOT honor ?grade=N (filter_grade stays None). This is by design but worth noting — admin viewers cannot see per-grade content_count. Counts in ContentManagement.jsx are computed client-side from /api/admin/content/items via effectiveContentParent, which is the intended fix.

Both admin_create_item and admin_update_item accept content_type as a free string with no enum/whitelist validation. Acceptable per design (per agent context note) but means typos like 'know_it_sheet' vs 'know-it-sheet' would silently persist. Consider a server-side allowlist or Pydantic Literal in the future.
test_credentials: Admin admin@learnersplanet.com / finlit@2026; Parent wallet_demo_parent / testpass123 (used for grade-scoped count verification)
seed_data_creation: Tests create and delete their own TEST_ prefixed content items; no permanent seed data left behind.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Login endpoint /api/auth/login uses field name 'identifier' (not 'username' or 'email'). Admin uses /api/auth/admin-login which returns 'session_token' (used as Bearer token). For grade-scoped content_count verification on /api/content/topics?grade=N, use a parent/teacher token — admin role bypasses filter_grade so admin queries cannot validate grade-scoped counts. Pick subtopics that satisfy both min_grade<=g<=max_grade for the chosen grade, otherwise the subtopic itself is filtered out of the response.
